人工智能转录创业公司Otter.ai今天宣布已在B轮融资中筹集了5,000万美元，其中包括1000万美元的可转换债券，并由Spectrum Equity领投。有了这些资金，该公司计划在明年通过其AI，深度学习，自然语言处理，前端和后端工程以及领导团队雇用员工，使员工人数增加两倍。
 音频转录市场的竞争不乏不足，据估计，到2025年，音频转录市场的价值将达到318.2亿美元。但是Otter.ai（前身为AISense）是语音到文本服务Otter背后的初创企业，已经设法为自己开拓了一个利基市场公司成立以来的五年内。随着Otter在超过230个国家/地区采用该技术，该公司的收入在2020年猛增800％。该公司表示，迄今为止已经录制了超过1亿次会议，涉及时间超过30亿分钟。
  Otter.ai由CEO Sam Liang和工程副总裁Yun Fu于2016年创立。Liang领导了Google定位团队，该团队在Google Maps应用程序上开发了“蓝点”，并创立了移动创业公司Alohar，该公司于2013年被阿里巴巴收购。
 Otter.ai的核心技术是由来自Google，Yahoo，Facebook，MIT，斯坦福，杜克大学和Cambridge的团队开发的，专门针对会话进行了优化。它可以使用称为区分的技术区分说话者，从而为每个人的声音生成独特的打印效果。转录在云中进行处理，并可以从Web，Dropbox或Otter.ai的iOS和Android设备移动应用程序中获取。在这里，可以搜索，复制和粘贴它们，滚动浏览，编辑和与每个记录顶部跟踪最常用术语的词云共享。
 “水獭的高精度是算法使应用程序能够“学习”的结果。”一位发言人告诉VentureBeat。 “这些广告在早期特别关注于具有多种口音的英语使用者-在美国范围内，并且经过优化以解决诸如亮语和地球上数十亿英语使用者的口音。梁总是对自然语言处理系统无法理解口音感到沮丧。”
 Otter.ai与Microsoft 365竞争，Microsoft 365可以举办具有AI支持功能的现场活动，例如与会者的面部识别和自动语音到文本转换，以及与Cisco和初创公司Voicera，Verbit，Trant，Simon的转录工具进行会议说，和Scribie。但是Otter.ai试图通过具有竞争力的价格来区别其服务。 Otter Pro的起价为每月$ 8.33，仅略高于该公司的业务层（每月$ 20）。 Otter.ai还提供了一个免费计划，每月有600分钟的转录时间和无限的云存储空间。
  Otter.ai最近通过Otter for Education将Otter引入了教育市场，该教育使教师可以控制对记录的成绩单的访问，并补充学生残疾服务和无障碍获取技术。 Otter.ai最近推出了Otter for Teams，这是一种订阅解决方案，旨在通过帐户管理，供应，报告和其他功能满足中小型企业的需求。今年夏天，Otter.ai首次推出了Otter for Events，该服务使用Otter的自然语言处理技术来捕获事件对话并将其实时转换为成绩单。 
Otter.ai提供适用于Zoom和Google Meet的插件，使与会人员可以实时获取会议的字幕，以及字幕。它是Otter.ai提供的Otter Live Notes的一部分，它使参与者可以直接从视频会议或会议后打开成绩单。 Otter Live Notes使用户可以访问Otter Voice Meeting Notes中可用的某些相同的实时功能，这些功能可以直接从视频会议平台启动。
 试点或积极使用Otter的组织不断增加，其中包括加利福尼亚州立大学，哥伦比亚大学和沃里克商学院。 Otter.ai声称拥有数百万用户，并预计这种大流行将继续推动增长。
 Lian通过电子邮件告诉VentureBeat：“如今，远程和混合工作正在成为趋势，这一点越来越明显。” “因此，我们需要重新考虑会议，以便使用生产力和协作工具使参加这个新的虚拟世界的每个人都更有效率。”
 除了Spectrum之外，Horizo​​ns Ventures，Draper Associates，GGV Ventures和Draper Dragon Fund也参与了Otter.ai的最新一轮融资。到目前为止，这家总部位于加利福尼亚州Lost Altos的公司的总融资额已超过6300万美元。

note_en:
AI transcription startup  Otter.ai today announced it has raised $50 million in a series B round that includes $10 million in convertible notes and was led by Spectrum Equity. With the funds, the company plans to triple its headcount over the next year as it hires across its AI, deep learning, natural language processing, frontend and backend engineering, and leadership teams.
 There’s no shortage of competition in the  audio transcription market, which is estimated to be worth $31.82 billion by 2025. But Otter.ai — formerly AISense — the startup behind speech-to-text service  Otter, has managed to carve out a niche for itself in the five years since its founding. The company’s revenue skyrocketed 800% in 2020 as Otter saw adoption in more than than 230 countries. And the company says it has transcribed over 100 million meetings, spanning 3 billion minutes to date.
  Otter.ai was founded by CEO Sam Liang and VP of engineering Yun Fu in 2016. Liang led the Google locations team that developed the “blue dot” on the Google Maps app and launched mobile startup Alohar, which was acquired by Alibaba in 2013.
 Otter.ai’s core technology, which was developed by a team hailing from Google, Yahoo, Facebook, MIT, Stanford, Duke, and Cambridge, is optimized for conversations. It can distinguish between speakers using a technique called diarization, generating a unique print for each person’s voice. Transcriptions are processed in the cloud and made available from the web, in Dropbox, or in Otter.ai’s mobile app for iOS and Android devices. There, they can be searched, copied and pasted, scrolled through, edited, and shared with a word cloud at the top of each recording that tracks most-used terms.
 “Otter’s high accuracy is a result of the algorithms that enable the app to ‘learn,'” a spokesperson told VentureBeat. “These were especially focused in the early days on English speakers with a variety of accents — regional within the U.S. and also optimized to address accents like Liang’s and the billions of English speakers on the planet. Liang had always been frustrated that accents were not understood by natural language processing systems.”
 Otter.ai competes with  Microsoft 365, which can host live events with AI-powered features such as facial recognition of attendees and autonomous speech-to-text conversion, as well as meeting transcription tools from  Cisco and startups  Voicera,  Verbit, Trint, Simon Says, and Scribie. But Otter.ai seeks to differentiate its service with competitive pricing. Otter Pro starts at $8.33 per month, making it only slightly pricier than the company’s business tier, which costs $20 per month. Otter.ai also offers a free plan, with 600 minutes of transcription per month and unlimited cloud storage.
  Otter.ai recently brought Otter to the education market with  Otter for Education, which lets instructors control access to recorded transcripts and complements student disability services and accessibility technologies. Otter.ai more recently launched  Otter for Teams, a subscription solution designed to accommodate the needs of small and medium-sized businesses with account management, provisioning, reporting, and other features. This summer, Otter.ai debuted Otter for Events, a service that uses Otter’s natural language processing tech to capture event conversations and turn them into transcripts in real time.
 Otter.ai offers plugins for Zoom and Google Meet that allow participants to get a live captioning of the meeting, followed by a transcript. It’s part of Otter Live Notes, Otter.ai’s offering that lets participants open a transcript directly from a videoconference or after a meeting. Otter Live Notes enables users to access some of the same real-time features available in Otter Voice Meeting Notes, which can be launched directly from a videoconferencing platform.
 The growing list of organizations that have piloted or are actively using Otter includes California State University, Columbia University, and the Warwick Business School. Otter.ai claims to have millions of users and expects that the pandemic will continue to fuel growth.
 “It is becoming more clear by the day that remote and hybrid working are trends that are here to stay,” Lian told VentureBeat via email. “Consequently, we need to rethink meetings in order to make them more productive for everyone participating in this new, virtual world with productivity and collaboration tools.”
 Beyond Spectrum, Horizons Ventures, Draper Associates, GGV Ventures, and Draper Dragon Fund participated in Otter.ai’s latest funding round. It brings the Lost Altos, California-based company’s  total raised to date to over $63 million.

title: 提供基于AI的转录服务的Otter.ai筹集了5000万美元的B轮融资，其中包括由Spectrum Equity领投的1000万美元可转换票据
title_en: Otter.ai, which offers an AI-based transcription service, raises $50M Series B, including $10M in convertible notes, led by Spectrum Equity

Facebook正在通​​过名为“好主意值得发现”的新广告系列继续其针对个性化广告的公开辩护。
 该公司表示，该广告系列旨在“帮助人们了解他们看到的个性化广告如何帮助他们发现自己喜欢的新事物并支持社区中的企业。”从星期四开始，它将出现在电视，广播和数字平台上。
  Facebook高管表示，该举措旨在支持在大流行中挣扎的小企业。
 但Facebook的另一个动机是：苹果公司正在改变iPhone，这有望颠覆移动广告领域，使广告商更难将广告定位到手机并跟踪其效果。这项变更实际上将为用户提供隐私选项，以共享他们的广告ID，即＆＃34; IDFA＆＃34;以前被深深地埋在用户中手机，并在打开应用程序时将其放在前面和中间。该公司在最近的一份收益报告中警告称，这一变化预计最快会在本季度损害Facebook的业务。
 Facebook高管们几个月来一直公开谴责苹果的改变。 Facebook甚至于去年下半年发起了平面广告活动，称这一改变将损害小型企业，并建议在线内容制造商将不得不转向订阅，以弥补失去的广告收入，迫使用户支付曾经免费的费用。
 当Apple的更改于初春生效时，Facebook将开始显示提示，鼓励用户共享其信息。该公司上个月开始测试提示，称Facebook使用该信息来提供更好的广告体验。
 新的广告系列在某种程度上旨在激发用户说“是”。到此提示。 
Facebook公司行销负责人安德鲁·斯特克（Andrew Stirk）表示，该公司的研究发现，有47％的小型企业担心他们在接下来的六个月内将无法生存，或者不确定他们会在未来的六个月内保持生存状态。 Covid-19大流行和随后的封锁已摧毁了从零售商到餐馆的所有事物。
  ＆＃34;目标是帮助人们了解个性化广告对小型企业的作用，其成长和蓬勃发展的能力，以及他们在帮助发现可能不存在的想法时所发挥的作用找到，＆＃34;他在本周的一次采访中告诉CNBC。
  该广告活动将在Facebook平台上和平台下进行12周。它包括由Droga5代理的电视节目，由David Wilson导演，由Grace Jones配音。它们以真实的业务为特色，并包含短语“＆＃34;然而，对于每一个大获好评的大创意，还有更多的它从未找到自己的名声。”
 电视广告的结尾处是文字“好主意”。个性化广告可帮助您找到它们。＆＃34;
  ＆＃34;我们要确保[小型企业]了解我们提供的工具，并尽可能使他们获得积极的体验，＆＃34;斯特克说。 ＆＃34;然后，对于消费者，我们希望提高对个性化广告在小型企业中的作用以及发现创意的能力的认识。
 该公司表示，它将提供更多有关个性化广告如何在Facebook和Instagram的仪表板上工作的信息，并且该广告系列的广告将投放到这些中心。在Facebook上，企业将能够发布带有#DeserveToBeFound的标签，而在Instagram上，将具有带有类似文本的标签。
 Facebook表示，它还试图使小型企业更容易开始营销。该公司对至少在今年6月之前使用Facebook在线购物平台的企业以及至少在8月之前进行的付费活动免收某些费用。 
竞选活动中着重介绍了生活方式品牌House of Takura的Annette Njau，与Facebook高管一起进行了简报。 她说，当前形式的个性化广告可以像更大的公司一样帮助她做广告，但担心苹果公司IDFA的变更会对此产生影响。  我现在不是路易威登或香奈儿...我们不能只是盲目地扔掉钱，＆＃34; 她说。 ＆＃34;因此，Facebook对广告所做的工作为我们提供了公平的竞争环境。
title: 在苹果公司IDFA变更之前，Facebook发起了电视，广播和数字广告活动，以支持名为“好主意”的个性化广告。
title_en: Facebook launches a TV, radio, and digital ad campaign in support of personalized ads called Good Ideas Deserve to be Found, ahead of Apple's IDFA changes

note:
在2017年夏天，Toelupe一家听说了犹他州Provo的一栋名为Encircle的小蓝屋。这家非营利组织传达了一个简单的信息：“没有边，只有爱”，在接下来的几年里，Encircle将会成为第二故乡，庇护所，也是迈卡·图卢普挽救生命的社区切入点。
  作为犹他州农村地区高中时期少数几个同性恋学生之一，米卡正处于抑郁，焦虑和社交孤立的困境中。这就是为什么步入Encircle的前门的感觉如此与众不同，在Encircle那里他感觉像是“十亿个之一”，而不是几个。
  “在这里产生的效果是如此的疯狂，”米卡说。 “确实确实挽救了我的生命。您可以走进房子，被爱着并尊重自己的人所包围。一直在那里，永远都欢迎您。”
  Encircle是一家位于犹他州的非营利组织，成立于2017年，旨在帮助LGBTQ +年轻人及其家人在全州社区资源机构中寻求支持和归属感。这个月，这家非营利组织发起了一场新的竞选活动，业务，音乐和技术领域的领导者齐聚一堂，苹果公司，Qualtrics创始人瑞安·史密斯（Ryan Smith）和他的妻子阿什利（Ashley）（也拥有犹他爵士队）以及Imagine Dragons的主唱提供了新的捐款。丹·雷诺兹（Dan Reynolds）和他的妻子，音乐家Aja Volkman。
  这笔价值400万美元的捐款将通过在犹他州，爱达荷州，内华达州和亚利桑那州建立新的社区资源机构，帮助启动Encircle的全国扩张。除了提供100万美元的捐款外，苹果还将捐赠iPad和其他产品，以帮助扩展Encircle的虚拟编程，并为数字连接，创造力和教育开辟新的途径。
    Encircle通过许多服务帮助年轻人及其家庭，包括免费和补贴的治疗课程，创建安全空间和促进社区的友谊圈，以及一项开放政策，帮助走过家门的每个人受到欢迎和接受。
  这种大流行意味着将许多此类服务在线上转移，但并没有减轻支持系统的紧迫性或重要性，该系统可以帮助与朋友隔绝的年轻人，常常比以往任何时候都更加孤立。技术捐赠将对非营利组织接触LGBTQ +青年的能力产生重大影响，从而有助于在“包围圈”房屋的四面墙之外建立社区。 
Encircle已经帮助了犹他州的无数家庭。克里斯安（Chrisann）和帕卡德·图勒普（Packard Toelupe）育有两个LGBTQ身份的孩子，他们的儿子米卡（Micah）和女儿克里斯蒂安（Khristian）。当克里斯蒂安（Khristian）与父母分享自己的性别认同时，Encircle帮助Toelupes找到了支持资源，并与其他希望建立社区的家庭联系起来。
  克里斯安说：“环抱使我有信心成为孩子的代言人，因为我看到很多其他父母都为孩子提倡”。 “起初这很寂寞。但是这里有我可以称呼的整个人际网络。您遇到了其他家庭，您意识到自己并不孤单。”
  Encircle正在帮助满足紧急需求。超过一半的LGBTQ青年正在与抑郁症作斗争，并且十分之四的LGBTQ +人报告在过去12个月中曾认真考虑过自杀。尽管如此，近一半的LGBTQ +年轻人表示，他们在过去的一年中无法获得心理健康咨询。
  Encircle不仅为年轻人，而且为他们的家庭扮演支持角色，为爱，理解和接受提供了空间。这家非营利组织以成为其服务社区的一部分而感到自豪，即使在LGBTQ +年轻人感到不自在或不敢分享自己的自我的地区，尤其如此。
      无论如何，Encircle的模式都奏效了-每年有10,000多名客人访问每个Encircle房屋。通过咨询服务，音乐之夜，社区服务项目和其他联谊活动，Encircle已成为面临孤独或沮丧的年轻人的重要生命线。
  幸运的是，Encircle于2017年在Provo开设了一所房子，距离他们的家仅20分钟路程。尽管有一些当地的LGBTQ +年轻人资源，但很少有人对他们的家人和整个犹他州的邻居产生如此深远的影响。
  当Toelupes第一次告诉他们的女儿关于“包围圈”时，Khristian在菲律宾执行任务。当她回到家中时，她发现了一个自己无法想象的支持系统，以及一个接受并支持她的人的社区。 
赫里斯蒂安说：“我只是在ba打。” “我想，‘哇，我不认为我能一次感受到所有的爱。’每次去围圈时，我都可以呼吸。我可以成为自己，而不必担心意见或我自己的安全。”
  Encircle的使命不仅是为家庭服务，而且是一个伟大的邻居和社区成员。这家非营利组织有助于增进对各种经验和身份的更深刻的理解和赞赏。根据米卡（Micah）的说法，学生一次开车花几个小时只是在Encircle房屋里待一会儿并不少见。
  “环抱产生了滚雪球效应，”米卡说。 “我开始前进，这挽救了我的生命，然后我学校的辅导员发现了这件事。它挽救了其他孩子的生命。友谊圈从第一组的两个人发展到房间里的20个孩子。”
  并非每个来访的人都得到家人的支持，但是Encircle专注于欢迎每个人，已使LGBTQ +年轻人拥有一系列经验，成为了生命线。萨凡纳·哈曼（Savannah Harman）在Encircle的盐湖城房屋中实习，在那里，Friendship Circles和其他社区建设活动帮助她找到了各行各业的支持朋友。
  “我找到了一个可爱的家庭，”萨凡纳说。 “由于Encircle是面向家庭的，因此即使您不带家人也可以为您提供空间。您会在那儿找到家人。”
    对于Micah而言，Encircle通过课程和展览帮助他获得了成为艺术家的信心和技能，从而与世界分享了他的作品。这些作品之一-水彩画-是一种自画像。米卡（Micah）的妈妈是一位热情的第一位顾客，而这件米卡（Micah）从未命名的物品现在挂在Toelupes的客厅里。
  克里斯安说：“每次走过去，我都会想，'成为'。” “因为就像弥迦那样成为自己。这就是Encircle对我所做的。他成长为弥迦–他的真实自我。”
note_en:
In the summer of 2017, the Toelupe family heard about a little blue house in Provo, Utah, called Encircle. The nonprofit had a simple message, “No sides, only love” — and in the years to come, Encircle would prove to be part second home, part sanctuary, and the entry point to a community Micah Toelupe credits with saving his life.
  As one of only a handful of gay students at his high school in rural Utah, Micah was struggling with depression, anxiety, and social isolation. That’s why stepping through the front doors of Encircle — where he felt like “one of a billion” instead of one of a few — felt so different.
  “The effect it had here is so insane,” says Micah. “It really did save my life. You could walk right into the house and be surrounded by people who are going to love and respect who you are. It was always there, and you were always welcome.”
  Encircle, a Utah-based nonprofit, was founded in 2017 to help young LGBTQ+ people and their families find support and a sense of belonging at community resource houses across the state. This month, the nonprofit launched a new campaign that leaders in business, music, and technology are rallying behind, with new donations from Apple, Qualtrics founder Ryan Smith and his wife, Ashley — who also own the Utah Jazz — and Imagine Dragons’s lead singer Dan Reynolds and his wife, musician Aja Volkman.
  The donations — valued at $4 million — will help kickstart Encircle’s national expansion, with new community resource houses in Utah, Idaho, Nevada, and Arizona. In addition to a $1 million contribution, Apple will be donating iPads and other products to help expand Encircle’s virtual programming and inspire new pathways for digital connection, creativity, and education.
    Encircle helps young people and their families through a number of services, including free and subsidized therapy sessions, Friendship Circles that create safe spaces and foster community, and an open door policy that helps everyone who walks through their doors feel welcomed and accepted.
  The pandemic has meant moving many of those services online, but it hasn’t dulled the urgency or importance of support systems that help young people who are cut off from their friends, often feeling more isolated than ever. The technology donations will make a meaningful difference in the nonprofit’s ability to reach LGBTQ+ youth where they are, helping create community beyond the four walls of an Encircle home.
    Encircle has already helped countless families across Utah. Chrisann and Packard Toelupe have two LGBTQ-identified children — their son Micah and their daughter, Khristian. When Khristian shared her gender identity with her parents, Encircle helped the Toelupes find supportive resources and connect with other families looking to build community.
  “Encircle gave me the confidence to be an advocate for my child, because I saw so many other parents being advocates for their children,” says Chrisann. “It was very lonely at first. But here was this whole network of people that I could call. You meet these other families, and you realize you’re not alone.”
  Encircle is helping to meet an urgent need. More than half of LGBTQ youth are battling symptoms of depression, and four out of 10 LGBTQ+ people report having seriously considered suicide in the past 12 months. Still, nearly half of LGBTQ+ young people say they’ve been unable to access mental health counseling over the last year.
  Encircle has taken on a supportive role not just for young people, but for their families — making space for love, understanding, and acceptance. The nonprofit prides itself on being a part of the communities it serves, even and especially in areas where LGBTQ+ youth feel uncomfortable or afraid to share their full selves.
      By any measure, Encircle’s model has worked — more than 10,000 guests visit each Encircle home every year. With counseling services, music nights, community service projects, and other bonding activities, Encircle has been a crucial lifeline to young people facing isolation or depression.
  Luckily for the Toelupes, Encircle opened a house in Provo in 2017, just 20 minutes from their home. And while there were some local resources for young LGBTQ+ people, few had such a profound impact on their family and neighbors across Utah County.
  When the Toelupes first told their daughter about Encircle, Khristian was on her mission in the Philippines. When she returned home, she found a support system unlike anything she could’ve imagined — and a community that accepted and supported her for who she is.
  “I was just bawling,” Khristian says. “I thought, ‘Wow, I didn’t think that I could feel all this love at once.’ Every time that I go to Encircle it’s time that I can actually breathe. I can just be — without having to worry about opinions or my own safety.”
  Encircle’s mission is not only to serve families but also be a great neighbor and community member. The nonprofit has helped foster a deeper understanding and appreciation for a diversity of experiences and identities. According to Micah, it isn’t uncommon for students to drive hours at a time just to spend a little while at an Encircle house.
  “Encircle had this snowball effect,” Micah says. “I started going and it saved my life, and then my counselors at my school found out about it. It was saving other kids’ lives. The Friendship Circles grew from two people in the first group to 20 kids in the room.”
  Not everyone who visits has the support of their family, but Encircle’s focus on welcoming everyone has made it a lifeline for LGBTQ+ youth with a range of experiences. Savannah Harman interned at Encircle’s Salt Lake City house, where Friendship Circles and other community-building activities helped her find supportive friends from all walks of life.
  “I found a lovely chosen family,” Savannah says. “Because Encircle is family oriented, it allows space for you even if you don’t bring your family. You will find your family there.”
    For Micah, Encircle helped him gain the confidence and skills to grow as an artist, with classes and exhibitions that helped him share his work with the world. One of those pieces — a watercolor painting — is something of a self-portrait. Micah’s mom was an enthusiastic first customer, and the piece, which Micah never named, now hangs in the Toelupes’ living room.
  “Every time I walk past it, I just think, ‘Becoming,’” Chrisann says. “Because it’s like Micah becoming himself. And that’s what Encircle, to me, did for him. He blossomed into Micah — his authentic self.”
title: 苹果帮助Encircle扩大对LGBTQ +青少年及其家人的支持
title_en: Apple helps Encircle expand its support for LGBTQ+ youth and their families

曾经有一段时间，谷歌是一家规模不大的古怪公司，其单一产品太棒了，以至于击败了竞争对手。那个时间早已不复存在。
 如今，谷歌是一家庞大的跨国大公司。但这有点低估了。可以将Google视为一种哥斯拉，它在一端收集有关其用户的数据，而在另一端收集金锭。它同时大规模地完成了这两项工作。
 在考虑Google时，有三件事不容易掌握，但它们非常重要：
 Google不是搜索引擎，也不只是生产力工具的制造商。 Google是一个巨大的广告平台。
  Google作为广告平台取得了惊人的惊人的成功。金锭以惊人的速度出现，并被用于其他各种用途。
  Google的成功，出色的做事，开出大量的金锭的事实，对我们和社会来说都是可怕的。
 在进入Google Analytics（分析）之前，让我们快速浏览一下这些要点。 
Google是一个广告平台。它所做的一切，所有的产品，都旨在销售广告。它的大多数产品都是免费的，其中许多是有用的，有的甚至是很棒的。但是它们的存在都是为了吸收更多的数据，以便在销售广告时变得更好。
  第2步：Google使用这些数据非常了解您：您喜欢什么，您不喜欢什么，是否怀孕，是否是同性恋，朋友住的地方，度过的时间，在线购买。
  第3步：Google利用对您的广泛了解，向您展示适合您的广告，就像量身定制的西装一样。
 这就是所谓的监控资本主义，当然不是要为您提供出色的用户体验，而是要赚钱。
 仅举一个我要提出的观点的完美例子：看看付费搜索结果（即广告）在Google的搜索结果中变得越来越难以识别的方式。
   对于一家热爱用户的公司，这并不是您期望看到的发展。这是您对一家希望获得更多利润的大型公司的期望。
  Google的许多产品都有绝对惊人的市场份额。 Google有九种产品，每种产品的用户超过十亿。谷歌浏览器是最受欢迎的网络浏览器，市场份额为64％。 Google的Android是移动设备上最受欢迎的操作系统，市场份额为72％。地球上大多数联网的人都在使用Google的产品。 
将此与Google垄断在线广告这一事实结合起来。好的，事实并非如此。它与Facebook都拥有双头垄断。 Facebook对我在这篇文章中提到的大多数事情都感到内gui，而且情况可能更糟，但是我将自己的毒液保存了一天。
 所有这些的结果是Google的收入令人massive目结舌：2020年约为1800亿美元，大约是新西兰的GDP。现在来看，仅在四年前，Google的收入就达到了900亿美元，这意味着收入在4年内翻了一番。哇！
 这座金锭山曾经流向报纸和杂志，但仅此而已。结果是本地新闻和杂志行业的淘汰。的确，在过去的三十年中，新闻机构在创新方面一直表现糟糕，如今，结果却一steam不振。为什么这样不好？继续阅读。
  Google的成功对Google及其股东有利。对我们来说，对消费者不利。我们生活在一个世界各地的新闻要么死于石头，要么幸存下来的世界。正如我们在过去几年中了解到的那样，运转良好的媒体对于民主至关重要。
 那么市场是否发挥了魔力并填补了空白？创造性破坏的创造部分发生了吗？我们现在是否有针对本地新闻的更好更好的解决方案？
 没有。在线新闻文章令人click目结舌，诱人点击标题，Cookie标语，隐私噩梦和广告重磅广告，这主要是Google的错。新闻制作人拼命扭动从内容中获得的每一个可悲的利润下降。
 您知道为什么在线食谱现在从作者的生平故事开始，并且在您获得真正的牛肉之前是无穷无尽的吗？是因为有Google。这是因为，即使是在线食谱的撰写者也在无所不能的在线上帝（即Google）面前自我推销。 
那一堆巨大的不断增长的金锭？这本身就是一个问题。
 “多年来，Google在网络搜索领域的垄断所产生的惊人资金激增了对其他多个细分市场的入侵，使Google能够与那些可能给数十亿人的生活带来更好体验的竞争对手一较高下。
  Google Apps和Google Maps在技术经济中均占有举足轻重的地位。他们是在为自己买单，还是将搜索广告收入用作火箭燃料？ Google以外的人都不知道。” –分手Google
 Google的规模和盈利能力还带来许多其他细微而又大的问题，在这里我只涉及了一些问题。
  Google正在收集所有产品的数据，那么为什么选择Google Analytics（分析）？因为对于大多数产品，如果您选择使用它们，则是收集到的数据。 Google Analytics（分析）不同。作为Web开发人员，您正在做出影响所有用户的选择。
 Google Analytics（分析）是最受欢迎的网站统计工具。网路上超过53％的网站都使用Google Analytics（分析）追踪访客。确实使用已知分析脚本的网站中有84％使用Google Analytics（分析）。这是网络上最受欢迎的第三方请求。它占所有网络请求的0.64％。” – Plausible.io博客
 刚开始开发网站时，很容易将Google Analytics（分析）添加到我创建的任何内容中。免费！很好！这就是每个人都在使用的！ 
它被许多浏览器（例如Firefox）阻止，因此数据不是很准确。  Google是否曾经在内部透露过Google Analytics（分析）数据的作用？ 没有。 但是我们甚至不需要推测。 对我来说，很明显，他们正在使用它来吞噬更多的数据并清除更多的金锭。  那么有其他选择吗？ 当然，有一堆，有些要花钱。 我认为值得花钱。 例如，这里有Plausible.io和Fathom（我在此博客中使用）和一整套自托管解决方案。  我认为，对于Web开发人员来说，考虑他们用于提供产品的“免费”工具是道义上的当务之急。 就Google Analytics（分析）而言，该工具只是表面上免费的。 我们都在支付隐藏的费用。  顺便说一句，我正在构建自己的自举应用程序。 在Twitter上关注我以保持更新。
note_en:
There was a time when Google was a small, quirky company with a single product so awesome that it blew away the competition. That time is long gone.
 These days Google is a gigantic multinational mega-corp. But that’s understating it a little. Think of Google as a kind of Godzilla that slurps up data about its users at one end and craps out gold ingots at the other. It does both of these at huge scale.
 When thinking about Google, there are three things that are not easy to grasp but which are immensely important:
 Google is not a search engine, it is not simply a maker of productivity tools. Google is a giant advertising platform.
  Google is spectacularly, awesomely, frighteningly successful as an advertising platform. The gold ingots are coming out at an alarming rate and being used for all kinds of other things.
  Google’s success, the fact that it is so good at what it does, the fact that it is crapping out a mountain of gold ingots, is terrible for us and society.
 Let’s take a quick tour of these points before getting onto Google Analytics.
  Google is an advertising platform. Everything it does, all of its products, are geared towards selling advertising. Most of its products are free, many of them are useful, and a few are even great. But they all exist to suck up more data so that it can become even better at selling advertising.
  Step 2: Google uses this data to get to know you very very well: what you like, what you don’t like, whether you are pregnant, whether you are gay, where your friends live, where you spend your time, what you purchase online.
  Step 3: Google uses its extensive knowledge of you to show you advertising that fits you like a tailor-made suit.
 It’s called  surveillance capitalism and it’s certainly not about giving you a great user experience, it’s about making money.
 To give just one perfect example of the point I’m making: look at the way that paid search results (i.e. adverts) have become ever harder to identify in Google’s search results.
   This is not the evolution you expect to see for a company that loves its users. This is what you expect from a mega-corp that wants ever more profits.
  Many of Google’s products have an absolutely staggering market share. Google has nine products with more than one billion users each. Google Chrome is the most popular web browser with a market share of 64%. Google’s Android is the most popular operating system on mobile devices with a market share of 72%. Google’s products are being used by most internet connected humans on earth.
 Combine this with the fact that Google has a monopoly on online advertising. Ok, actually that’s not true. It has a duopoly along with Facebook. Facebook is guilty of most of the things I mention in this post, and is probably worse, but I’m saving my venom for Facebook for another day.
 The result of all this is that Google’s revenue is eye-wateringly massive: around 180 billion USD in 2020, which is around the GDP of New Zealand. Now consider that only four years ago, Google’s revenue was 90 billion USD, which means that the revenue doubled in 4 years. Wow!
 This mountain of gold ingots used to flow to newspapers and magazines – but no more. The result has been the decimation of local news and the magazine industry. It is true that news organizations have been terrible at innovation in the past three decades and now they have been steam-rollered as a result. Why is this bad? Read on.
  Google’s success is good for Google and its shareholders. It’s not good for us, the consumers. We live in a world where local news is either stone dead or barely surviving. As we have learned in the past few years, a functioning media is essential to democracy.
 So has the market done its magic and filled the gap? Has the creation part of  creative destruction happened? Do we now have a better and superior solution for local news?
 Nope. Online news articles are a hideous cluster-fuck of click-baiting headlines, cookie banners, privacy nightmares and top heavy with advertising and it’s mostly Google’s fault. Producers of news are desperately wringing every last pathetic drop of profit from their content.
 Do you know why online recipes now begin with the author’s life story and are an interminable bore until you reach the actual beef? It’s because of Google. It’s because even writers of online recipes are prostrating themselves before the omnipotent online God that is Google.
 And that huge growing pile of gold ingots? That’s in itself a problem.
 “For many years, the astonishing torrent of money thrown off by Google’s Web-search monopoly has fueled invasions of multiple other segments, enabling Google to bat aside rivals who might have brought better experiences to billions of lives.
  Google Apps and Google Maps are both huge presences in the tech economy. Are they paying for themselves, or are they using search advertising revenue as rocket fuel? Nobody outside Google knows.” –  Break up Google
 There are many other nuanced and large problems created by Google’s size and profitability and I’ve only scratched the surface here.
  Google is harvesting data across all of its products, so why pick on Google Analytics? Because for most of the products, if you choose to use them, it’s your data that is harvested. It’s different for Analytics. You as a web developer are making a choice that affects all of your users.
 Google Analytics is the most popular website stats tool. More than 53% of all sites on the web track their visitors using Google Analytics. 84% of sites that do use a known analytics script use Google Analytics. It’s the most popular third-party request on the web. It accounts for 0.64% of all network requests.” –  Plausible.io blog
 When I first began to develop websites, it was a no-brainer to add Google Analytics to anything I created. It’s free! It’s good! It’s what everyone uses!
  It’s blocked by many browsers (e.g. Firefox) so the data is not very accurate.
  Has Google ever revealed what it does with data from Analytics internally? Nope. But we don’t even need to speculate. It seems pretty obvious to me that they’re using it to guzzle up even more data and to crap out ever more gold ingots.
 So are there alternatives? Sure, there’s a bunch and some cost money. I think it’s money worth spending. For instance, there’s  Plausible.io and there’s  Fathom (which I use on this blog) and a whole load of self-hosted solutions.
 I believe it is a moral imperative for web developers to think about the “free” tools they are using to provide their products. In the case of Google Analytics, the tool is only very superficially free. We are all paying the hidden costs.
   By the way, I’m building my  own bootstrapped app. Follow me  on Twitter to keep updated.
title: Google Analytics（分析）：停止喂食野兽
title_en: Google Analytics: Stop feeding the beast

        Jean-Marc Jancovici是法国政府气候变化和能源顾问，也是法国气候高级理事会的成员。他是Carbon 4的创始合伙人，Carbon 4是一家总部位于巴黎的数据咨询公司，专门从事低碳转型和气候变化的物理风险（www.carbone4.com）。他还是位于巴黎的智囊团倡导低碳经济的The Shift Project的创始人兼总裁（www.theshiftproject.org）。 Jean-Marc Jancovici还担任Mines ParisTech的副教授。
  由于资源的开采和环境的变化，我们社会的热工业发展已成为可能。不幸的是，它导致了人类在全球范围内遭受的严重环境后果：不断变化和不可预测的气候，极端天气事件以及生物多样性的崩溃。人类正在为技术和技术进步承担后果。因此，技术能否仍使我们免受气候变化的影响？ Jean-Marc Jancovici将通过能量范式解决这个问题。他将首先详细介绍现代社会是如何围绕热能和核能构造的，然后将讨论这种构造对全球气候和社会的影响。最后，Jean-Marc Jancovici将探讨经济增长与可持续气候管理之间的取舍。
note_en:
Update (2/23/21): A recording of the talk has been uploaded at the bottom of this page.
        Jean-Marc Jancovici is an advisor to the French government on climate change and energy as part of the French High Council for Climate. He is a founding partner of Carbon 4, a Paris-based data consultancy specializing in low carbon transition and the physical risks of climate change ( www.carbone4.com). He is also the founder and president of The Shift Project, a Paris-based think tank advocating for a low carbon economy ( www.theshiftproject.org). Jean-Marc Jancovici also serves as an associate professor at Mines ParisTech.
  The thermo-industrial development of our society has been possible due to resource extraction and the transformation of our environment. Unfortunately, it has led to severe environmental consequences that humanity is experiencing around the globe: shifting and unpredictable climate, extreme weather events, and biodiversity collapse. Humanity is paying the consequences for technical and technological progress. Thus, can technology still save us from climate change? Jean-Marc Jancovici will address this question through the paradigm of energy. He will first detail how modern society is structured around thermal and nuclear energies, and will then discuss the impact of this structure on global climate and society. Finally, Jean-Marc Jancovici will conclude by exploring the trade-offs between economic growth and sustainable climate stewardship.
title: 技术会拯救我们免受气候变化的影响吗？
title_en: Will technology save us from climate change?

  我们已经确定这些较旧的控制台都是基于图块的（除了Atari或其他非常老的硬件除外），但是它们并没有共享相同的格式。磁贴可以有多种格式，具体取决于我们所讨论的颜色和系统。因此，与其立即开始撰写文档，不如我写了一些非常好的文档来帮助您理解控制台图形。您还将需要您的第一个实用程序！您将需要图块编辑器。瓷砖编辑器很多，但是最简单的一种瓷砖编辑器就是Tile Layer Pro。
  *重要说明*虽然使用我们今天可用的工具通常可以很容易地进行图形黑客入侵，但某些游戏以压缩格式存储其图形，您将无法在图块编辑器中查看或编辑它们。这些游戏需要更高级的步骤来处理，这超出了本节的范围。如果您想了解有关压缩的更多信息，可以在“文档”部分中找到大量信息。
      图形黑客-这是由我们的常驻员工专家RedComet编写的文档！本文档是您学习Graphics Hacking所需的全部内容。该文档甚至可以给您一些实践经验！
  如果您希望在游戏中进行任何形式的文本编辑并且超越许多黑客仅做过的曲奇切割器图形，表格是ROMhacking知识中另一个必不可少的知识。进行任何形式的翻译工作也绝对需要表。如您所见，这是一个非常重要的知识。许多新手有时难以掌握表格的概念，我认为其中很大一部分可能是由于不了解十六进制！在制作使用数字的表之前，一定要了解要处理的数字的类型！ 
那么，什么是桌子呢？表格是一个简单的文本文件，列出了游戏使用的所有字体字符，以及十六进制（或二进制）代码在ROM中代表它们的含义。为什么需要一个？因为您不能打开ROM来查看文本（在极少数情况下，文本以ASCII存储，例如您现在正在阅读的文本除外）。表是翻译的关键步骤，因此您可以在ROM中查看和使用文本。这里有一些很好的文档，可以更好地解释这个概念以及如何制作自己的概念。
    如何制作表文件-有关如何制作表文件的简短简短指南。
  假人用桌-更详细的制表指南，其中也涵盖了日本制表。
  ROM黑客表权威指南-一个冗长的文档，解释了很多表的制作，但是，某些推荐的实用程序如Nesticle就是旧的。
  一般来说，翻译黑客只是ROMhacking的一种特殊形式。翻译显然主要涉及文本编辑。我希望您已经阅读并掌握了表格的内容，因为如果您希望进行翻译，则需要了解它们。进行翻译的主要区别在于表格是另一种语言！我们该如何处理？我们处理任何其他表的方式相同。
  每种语言都有某种字母。花几分钟在Google上阅读一些有关您要翻译的语言的信息可能是一个好主意。没错，黑客应该花一些时间来熟悉源语言中的一些基本概念，即使实际上并不需要翻译这些脚本。您不需要阅读任何东西，但是您应该了解语言字母和其他一些基本知识。 Google是您的朋友，请不要害怕使用它！
  人们翻译的大多数主机游戏都是日语。这也是我最熟悉的翻译类型，因此我将以它为例。但是，这些概念可以应用于任何语言。日语使用两个基本字母，即平假名和片假名。您将需要知道这些日语字母的顺序，就像制作日语表格的英语字母一样。 
只要您的游戏没有压缩字体，您就可以通过将日文图块编辑为英文字母来简化生活，从而进行一些相对搜索并开始获取初始表值。日语引入的一项新功能是双字节表值。使用汉字（第三组日语字符）的任何游戏都将为表中的每个条目使用两个字节。这是因为有超过255个汉字！其实有几千！幸运的是，大多数游戏只使用几百到一千左右。还要注意，这样的表可以同时使用两个字节和一个字节的值。汉字两个字节，其他所有字节。因此，事情可能会有些棘手。
  借助我们到目前为止所学的知识和这些其他资源，您应该能够尝试翻译。
    假人用桌-更详细的制表指南，其中也涵盖了日本制表。
  JWPce-免费的日语文字处理器。这是在计算机上查看日语的最简单方法。
      TaBuLar-一个很好的便捷表生成器，甚至可以在某种程度上生成日文表。
  多亏了一些非常有才华的人，现在完全可以在没有任何ROMhacking经验的情况下编辑图形，关卡甚至您喜欢的游戏的声音！当我开始这个兴趣爱好时，还没有这样的东西，所以要非常感谢这些人花时间为您制作这些实用程序。
  但是，这些实用程序的缺点是它们是特定于游戏的，并且仅当您正在开发的特定游戏为它们制作了其中一种实用程序时才有用。无论如何，我觉得在本节中需要提到它们，因为也许这些确实是您需要采取的方式，并且真正的ROMhacking可能不是您的最佳选择。您可以查看这些类似于RPG制造商的产品。有些人可以编写自己的RPG，而另一些人则使用RPG制造商作为实现梦想的唯一手段。 
链接到我们数据库中的游戏编辑器！ -这是我们当前提供的游戏编辑器。
  要制作真正高级的ROMhack并使其比大多数明亮，您可能需要考虑编辑音乐。在大多数情况下，编辑音乐是一项更高级的任务。但是，如果您是真正快速学习的人之一，那么您也许可以很快地掌握这些知识。我们在本节中包括音乐编辑，以使本节至少在一定程度上涵盖ROMhacking的所有领域，并至少向人们介绍所有内容。
      Dragon Warrior Bytes-详细指南，概述了将音乐入侵Dragon Warrior的过程。适合快速学习的初学者。
  其他一些不太高级的音乐黑客文档-以下是我们数据库中一些其他的文档，它们是针对中级黑客的，但它们可能适合快速学习的初学者。
  这里有一些专门针对新手的优秀文档。这些是绝对适合初学者的最佳数据库常规文档。这些文档建立在我们在本节中学到的概念的基础上。现在，您应该能够进入这些文档并了解其中的内容。
  如果您已阅读并理解了这些文档，则准备好自己进入我们的数据库！恭喜，您已经成功入门！祝你好运！
  ROMhacking的艺术-Vagla撰写的许多主题的不错的初学者入门指南。 
面向初学者的ROM黑客权威指南-本文档以问题/答案类型格式涵盖了各种主题。 它涵盖了许多其他地方未涵盖的小技巧。  每个出色的ROMhacker背后都有一些出色的工具！ ROMhacking.net人员推荐以下一些工具来帮助您入门。 这些不一定是最好的实用程序，但是它们是最好的实用程序，它足够简单，可以用于像您这样的新人。  在您积累了一些经验之后，请随时自行进入我们的实用程序数据库，并尝试使用我们的250多个实用程序！  JWPce-免费的日语文字处理器。 这是在计算机上查看日语的最简单方法。  TaBuLar-一个很好的便捷表生成器，甚至可以在某种程度上生成日文表。
note_en:
So, you want to be a ROMhacker? If you're reading this, you're probably overwhelmed at the large number of utilities and documents available on this site. What should you be looking for? What do you need? Where do you start? 	This section will try and help clarify these things for you.
  It doesn't matter if you're going to try and do translations, graphical hacks, or level hacks, you really need the same fundamental knowledge for any kind of ROMhacking. We have taken the time to label all the documents and utilities in our database that we feel are helpful for beginners just like you. However, sometimes these beginner documents still don't quite start at the beginning. That's what we're going to do here.
  We're going to show you around and point you to what you need to know to start helping yourself with all our beginner level documents on your own! After all, ROMhacking is very much a self taught hobby. Here is an outline of what we'll be seeing on our tour:
      If at all through this section, you get stuck on any terminology, feel free to look it up in our  ROMhacking.net Dictionary of ROMhacking Terms!
  The first thing you're going to need to know before you do much of anything is a little bit about binary and hexadecimal(shorthand form of binary). Binary is the fundamental building block of the computer and thus, it inherently is the building block of ROMhacking. Binary is the language of machines. But, most of us don't want to have to write out every single 1 and 0 on a machine, so we use what's called 'Hexadecimal'.
  Our resident expert Neil has written a nice document for you to read and gain the knowledge you need in this area. Since everybody learns differently, we have also linked a few other sources that may help explain the same topics better. Between all of them, you should come out with the basic knowledge you need. Remember, help is always available in our forums if you have questions. But please, read all of the material first before asking questions.
    Hexadecimal(The Basics of Bases) - Our resident staff member Neil has written this document to teach you what you need to know!
  Hex Lesson - A lesson on hex from the perspective of a newer person who has just learned it.
  Now that we've gotten some basic math at of the way, let's jump right into the fun stuff! Most consoles that came before the Playstation era are what we call 'tile based' systems. What's a tile? Well, let's start with a pixel first. Most of you probably already know what a pixel is. A pixel is one tiny dot on your TV or computer monitor. Look real close at your TV or monitor and you can see them. These older consoles can't draw individual pixels(from the game creator's perspective). The smallest thing these systems can put on screen is an 8x8 pixel square area called a 'tile'. You should also understand that text is drawn to the screen the exact same way graphics are. There is no magical difference between text and graphics in a video game consoles. This is even true with today's systems. The routines used to get text on the screen are essentially specialized graphics routines.
  Newer systems can use a variety of ways to display graphics ranging from 3D polygons to standard 2D type images. These are generally more complex. That is beyond the scope of this section, but you can find information on Playstation graphics formats in our  Documents section.
  We've established that these older consoles are all tile based(with exception of Atari or other extremely old hardware), however they don't all share the same format for their tiles. The tiles can come in a variety for formats depending on the colors and system we're talking about. So, rather than me start writing a document right now, here are some very good ones already written to help you understand console graphics. You're also going to need your first utility! A tile editor is what you'll need. There are many tile editors, but one of the simplest to use to start with is  Tile Layer Pro.
  *IMPORTANT NOTE* While graphics hacking is generally pretty easy with the tools we have available today, some games store their graphics in a compressed format and you will not be able to see or edit them in tile editors. These games require more advanced steps to handle that are beyond the scope of this section. If you want more information on compression, you can find plenty of information in our  Documents section.
      Graphics Hacking - Here is a document written by our resident staff expert RedComet! This document is all you need to learn the ropes on Graphics Hacking. This document will even give you some hands on experience!
  Tables are another essential piece of ROMhacking knowledge you'll need if you ever hope to do any sort of text editing in your game and move beyond the cookie cutter graphics only hacks many have done. Tables are also absolutely required for doing any sort of Translation effort. As you can see, this is a very important thing to learn. Many newcomers have trouble grasping the table concept sometimes and I feel much of that can be contributed to not understanding hexadecimal! It's important that you understand the type of numbers you're dealing with before making a table that uses them!
  So, what exactly is a table? A table is a simple text file that lists all the font characters the game uses, and what hexadecimal (or binary) code represents them in the ROM. Why do you need one? Because you can't just open up a ROM and see the text (except in rare cases where the text is stored in ASCII such as the text you're reading now). A table is the key step that acts as a translation so that you can see and work with the text in a ROM. Here are several good documents explaining this concept better and how to make your own.
    How to Make Table Files - A short simple guide to how to make table files.
  Tables For Dummies - A more detailed table making guide which also covers Japanese table making.
  The Definitive Guide to ROM Hacking Tables - A lengthy document which explains a lot of table making, however, some recommended utilities are old such as Nesticle.
  Translation hacking is simply a specialized form of ROMhacking in general. Translations obviously deal primarily with text editing. I hope you've read and gotten the hang of tables because you'll need to understand them if you ever hope to do a translation. The key difference in doing a translation is the table is in another language! How do we handle that? The same exact way we handle any other table.
  Every language has some sort of alphabet. It would probably be a good idea to take a few minutes on Google and read a little bit about the language you want to translate from. That's right, the hacker should take some time to get familiar with some basic concepts in the source language even if they aren't actually going to translate the script. You don't need to be able to read anything, but you should know about the alphabet of your language and some other basic things. Google is your friend, don't be afraid to use it!
  Most console games that people translate are in Japanese. That's also the type of translation I'm most familiar with, so I will use that as an example. However, these concepts can apply to any language. Japanese uses two basic alphabets, Hiragana, and Katakana. You're going to need to know the order of these Japanese alphabets just as you would the English alphabet to make a Japanese table.
  As long as your game doesn't have a compressed font, you can make life much easier for yourself by editing the Japanese tiles to English alphabet just so you can do some relative searching and get the initial table values started. One new thing introduced with Japanese is double byte table values. Any game that uses Kanji(a third set of Japanese characters) will use two bytes for each entry in the table. This is because there are more than 255 Kanji! In fact, there are a few thousand! Luckily, most games only use a few hundred to about a thousand or so. Also note that tables like this can use BOTH two byte AND one byte values. Two bytes for Kanji and one byte for everything else. So, things can get a little tricky.
  You should be able to get your feet wet in trying to do a translation with the knowledge we've learned so far and these additional resources.
    Tables For Dummies - A more detailed table making guide which also covers Japanese table making.
  JWPce - A free Japanese word processor. This is the easiest way to view Japanese on your computer.
      TaBuLar - A nice handy table generator that can even generate Japanese tables to some extent.
  Thanks to some very talented individuals, it is now possible to edit graphics, levels, and even sound of your favorite game without any ROMhacking experience at all! When I started in this hobby, no such things existed, so truly be thankful that these people took the time to make these utilities for you.
  The drawback to these utilities however, is they are game specific and are only useful if a particular game your working on has one of these utilities made for them. In any case, I feel they needed to be mentioned in this section because maybe these are really the way you need to go and real ROMhacking might not be your cup of tea. You can view these similar to RPG makers. Some people can code their own RPG, while others use RPG makers as the only means to bring their dream to life.
  Link to the Game Editors in our database! - this is our current offerings of game editors.
  To make a really advanced ROMhack and make it shine brighter than most, you may want to consider editing the music. In most cases, editing music is a more advanced task. However, if you're one of those people who is a really quick learner, you just might be able to pick this stuff up quickly. We've included music editing in this section to make the section complete in covering all the areas of ROMhacking to at least some degree and at least give people introduction to everything.
      Dragon Warrior Bytes - A detailed guide outlining hacking the music to Dragon Warrior. Suitable for quick learning beginner.
  Some other less advanced music hacking documents - Here are some other documents in our database for a more intermediate hacker, but may be suitable for a quick learning beginner.
  Here are some good documents aimed specifically at the newest of newbies. These are the best general documents in our database for absolute beginners. These documents build on the concepts we have learned in this section. You should now be able to move into these documents and understand what's there.
  If you've read and understand these documents, you're ready to go off into our database by yourself! Congratulations, you've successfully gotten started! Good Luck!
  The Art of ROMhacking - A nice general beginner's guide to many topics by Vagla.
  The Definitive Guide to ROM Hacking for Complete Beginners - This document covers a variety of topics in a question/answer type format. It covers a lot of small tips not covered in other places.
  Behind every great ROMhacker are some great tools! Here are some tools to help you get started recommended by the ROMhacking.net staff. These are not necessarily the best utilities, but they are the best utility that is simple enough to be used to new people like yourself.
  After you've gained a bit of experience, feel free to move out into our Utility database by yourself and try out several of our 250+ utilities!
  JWPce - A free Japanese word processor. This is the easiest way to view Japanese on your computer.
      TaBuLar - A nice handy table generator that can even generate Japanese tables to some extent.
﻿﻿

如果您还没有注意到我真的很喜欢纯文本，那么我想我会发现一些在不同软件系统中使用的纯文本协议。 Redis协议规范通过描述其自己的协议来最好地说明了这一点，但这适用于我将介绍的所有纯文本协议。
  下次构建软件系统并必须交换数据时，首先使用简单的纯文本协议，然后增加复杂性以供日后优化。所有这些协议都可以作为纯文本数据通过TCP或UDP发送。它们易于检查，体积小且直接。
  从列表中最简单的一个开始是Graphite纯文本协议，该协议用于通过Carbon将度量标准传递到Graphite。
       列表中下一个最简单的是Influx Line Protocol。尽管这与Graphite相似，但确实为其规范添加了一些其他元数据。
       这是事情变得更复杂的地方，但是上面的三个相同的特征仍然适用。为了简洁起见，为了简化协议，我们看到该协议是一行文本，并且初始命令规定了在其后允许使用哪些自变量数据类型。
     该特定协议还有更多内容可以用官方规范中概述的文本格式表示字符串，数字，数组等。
   是的，HTTP只是纯文本。由于协议本身具有大量的元数据排列，因此让我们来看一些HTTP请求和响应的示例。 
HTTP / 1.1 200 OKAccept-Ranges：bytesContent-Length：6262Content-Type：text / html; charset = utf-8日期：2020年9月2日，星期三01:41:35 GMTLast修改：2020年9月2日，星期三01:37:33 GMT
 当您访问http://blainsmith.com/articles/plain-text-protocols/时，浏览器将构造请求，并在从服务器返回响应时解析响应。允许的标头还有很多，但是该协议的基础非常简单。
   全世界都在使用这些类型的纯文本协议，因此，如果您发现自己正在构建软件系统，并且有可用的协议，请不要重新发明轮子。如果现有的纯文本协议可以适应您的目的，那么请这样做，因为可能已经有一个库可以用于对该协议进行序列化和反序列化，因此您不必编写自己的协议。如果这些协议中的任何一个都不满足您的需要，并且您需要编写自己的协议，那么请保持简单。如果每个人的Web浏览器都使用了已经使用了数十年的HTTP，那么除非您可以证明没有其他必要，否则没有理由创建复杂的协议。
note_en:
If you haven't noticed already I really love plain text so I thought I would find some plain text protocols that are used in different software systems. The Redis Protocol specification states it best by describing its own protocol, but this applies to all of the plain text protocols I will cover.
  The next time you are building a software system and have to exchange data start with using a simple plain text protocol and then add in the complexity for optimization later. All of these protocols can be sent as plain text data over a TCP or UDP. They are easily inspected, small, and direct.
  Starting off with the simplest one in the list is the Graphite plaintext protocol for delivering metrics into Graphite via Carbon.
       The next simplest one in the list is the Influx Line Protocol. While this is similar to Graphite it does add some additional metadata to its specification.
       Here is where things get more complex, but the same 3 characteristics from above apply still. Simplifying the protocol for the sake of brevity in this article we see that the protocol is a single line of text and the initial command dictates what argument data types are allowed after it.
     There is more to this particular protocol to denote strings, numbers, arrays, etc in a text format which is outlined in the official specification.
   Yes, HTTP is just plain text. Since the protocol itself has lots of permutations of metadata lets just look at some examples of an HTTP request and response.
    HTTP/1.1 200 OKAccept-Ranges: bytesContent-Length: 6262Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8Date: Wed, 02 Sep 2020 01:41:35 GMTLast-Modified: Wed, 02 Sep 2020 01:37:33 GMT<THE HTML OF THIS ARTICLE>
 Your browser constructs the request when you visit  http://blainsmith.com/articles/plain-text-protocols/ and parses the response when it comes back from the server. There are a lot more allowable headers, but the basics of this protocol are pretty straightforward.
   The world runs on these types of plain text protocol so don't reinvent the wheel if you find yourself building a software system and there is an existing protocol available. If there is an existing plain text protocol that you can adapt to your purposes then do that since there might be a library available already to serialize and deserialize the protocol so you don't have to write your own. If any of these protocols do not fit your needs and you need to write your own then keep it simple. If everyone's web browsers are using HTTP which has worked for decades there is no reason for you to create a complicated protocol unless your can prove the need to otherwise.
title: 纯文本协议

note:
假设存在外星人，并想象其中有些人一直在注视着我们的地球长达整整八年半的时间。他们会看到什么？在这段宽广的时间跨度中，大部分时间是在逐渐缓慢地变化着。大洲漂流了；冰盖起伏不定；连续的物种出现，进化，其中许多物种已灭绝。
 但是，在地球历史的一小部分（最后一百个世纪）中，植被的变化速度比以前快得多。这标志着农业的开始以及后来的城市化。随着人口的增加，变化加速了。
 然后出现了更快的更改。在短短一个世纪之内，大气中二氧化碳的数量开始以危险的速度迅速上升。出现了无法用自然过程解释的无线电发射，并且发生了其他前所未有的事情：从行星表面发射的火箭完全逃离了生物圈。一些航天器被推进到环绕地球的轨道；其他人则前往月球，火星，木星，甚至冥王星。
 如果那些假想的外星人继续保持警惕，他们在下个世纪会见证什么？由于气候变化，在活动的最后痉挛之后是否会保持沉默？还是地球的生态稳定？会有大量的地形吗？从地球发射的宇宙飞船是否会在其他地方产生新的生命绿洲？
  让我们具体考虑一下太空探索的未来。诸如维京号，卡西尼号，新视野号，朱诺号和罗塞塔号等成功任务都是上世纪技术完成的。我们可以现实地期望，在本世纪中，整个机器人系统（行星，卫星和小行星）将被机器人飞船探索。
  不可否认，NASA的新恒心漫游者在火星Jezero陨石坑上飞驰可能会错过一些令人震惊的发现，这是人类地质学家无法合理忽略的。但是机器学习和传感器技术也在迅速发展。相比之下，人员和自主任务之间的成本差距仍然很大。
 我们认为，载人航天的未来取决于SpaceX和Blue Origin等私人资助的冒险家，他们准备参加降价计划，其风险远高于西方国家对公共支持项目施加的风险。这些创举-将硅谷式文化带入了长期以来由NASA和少数航空航天集团主导的领域-革新和改进了火箭技术，其速度远远超过NASA或欧洲航天局。国家机构的未来作用将被削弱-更像是机场而不是航空公司。 
我们认为，具有领导力的私营公司应领导所有涉及人类的降价高风险企业任务。仍然会有许多志愿者-甚至有一些甚至接受单程票-由与早期探险家和登山者相同的动机驱动。应避免使用“太空旅游”一词。它使人们相信这种风险是例行且低风险的。如果是这样的话，那么不可避免的事故将像航天飞机一样严重。这些漏洞必须以危险，极限运动或无畏探索的形式出售。
 太空飞行的最关键障碍来自化学燃料的内在效率低下，以及要求运载的燃料重量远远超过有效载荷的重量。只要我们依靠化学燃料，行星际旅行仍将是一个挑战。核电可能具有变革性。允许更高的航向速度将大大减少太阳系的传播时间，不仅减少了宇航员的无聊，而且减少了他们受到有害辐射的影响。如果可以在地面上供应燃料，效率会更高；例如，通过“太空电梯”将太空船推进到轨道，然后使用地球上产生的“星光”型激光束推动附着在太空船上的“帆”。
 到2100年，费利克斯·鲍姆加特纳（Felix Baumgartner）（2012年打破高空气球自由落体的奥地利跳伞运动员）造型的寻求刺激的人可能已经在火星或什至小行星上建立了基地。埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）表示，他想在火星上死去，“但不影响生命”。这是一个现实的目标，并且是一对诱人的目标。
 但是不要指望地球有大规模的移民。认为太空逃避了地球的问题是一种危险的幻想。我们必须在这里解决这些问题。应对气候变化或COVID-19大流行看似令人生畏，但与改造火星相比，这简直是小菜一碟。我们的太阳系中没有任何地方可以提供像南极洲或珠穆朗玛峰顶这样的环境。简而言之，普通风险厌恶者没有B行星。
 尽管如此，我们（以及我们在地球上的后代）仍应为勇敢的太空冒险家们欢呼。在引领后人类的未来并确定22世纪及以后发生的事情方面，它们发挥着关键作用。
  先锋探险者将不适应他们的新栖息地，因此他们将具有令人信服的动机来重新设计自己。他们将利用将在未来数十年内开发的超强大的遗传和机器人技术。这可能是迈向新物种的第一步。
 有机生物需要一个可以在其中出现和进化的行星表面环境。但是，如果后人类过渡到完全无机的情报，他们将不需要气氛。他们甚至可能更喜欢零重力，尤其是在构造大量文物时。在深空，非生物的大脑可能会发展出人类甚至无法想象的力量。 
有机大脑的大小和处理能力受到化学和代谢限制。也许我们已经接近这些限制。但是，没有这样的限制适用于或约束电子计算机（甚至更少，也许是量子计算机）。因此，通过“思维”的任何定义，有机人类型大脑可以实现的数量和强度将被AI的大脑淹没。
 我们也许快到达尔文进化论的尽头了，但是智能生物的技术进化才刚刚开始。它可能发生在离地球最快的地方，尽管我们的生存可能取决于确保地球上的AI保持“仁慈”，但我们并不希望（当然也不会希望）地球上人类的如此迅速的变化。
  毫无疑问，机器将逐渐超越或增强我们越来越多的独特人类能力。分歧只是关于发生这种情况的时间表。发明家和未来主义者Ray Kurzweil表示，这将是几十年的事情。更谨慎的科学家设想了几个世纪。无论哪种方式，技术进步的时标都与导致人类崛起的达尔文进化的时标相比是即时的，更重要的是，它还不到未来宇宙时间广阔范围的百万分之一。未来技术发展的产物可能会超过人类，甚至超过我们的粘液霉菌。
  哲学家和计算机科学家争论着意识是否仅能表征人，猿和狗所拥有的湿有机大脑的类型。电子情报，即使他们的智力看起来超人，也会缺乏自我意识吗？能够想象不存在的事物的能力？内心的生活？还是意识是任何足够复杂的网络最终都会拥有的新兴属性？有人说这是无关紧要的，例如询问潜水艇是否会游泳。
  我们认为不是。如果这些机器被计算机科学家称为“僵尸”，那么我们将不会赋予他们与我们相同的经验值，而人类后的未来似乎将显得黯淡无光。另一方面，如果他们有意识，我们应该欢迎他们未来霸权的前景。
 如果他们成为完全自主的实体，他们的指导动机将是什么？我们必须承认我们绝对不知道。想想过去曾经推动人类奋斗的各种奇怪动机（意识形态，金融，政治，自我主义和宗教动机）。这是一个简单的示例，说明它们与我们的天真期望有何不同：它们可以是沉思的。甚至不那么引人注目，他们可能会意识到在低温下思考更容易，因此它远离任何恒星，甚至冬眠数十亿年，直到宇宙微波背景降温到远低于目前的3开氏度。在另一方面，他们可能是扩张主义者，这似乎是大多数考虑过未来文明轨迹的人的期望。
 即使生命仅起源于地球，它也不必保持宇宙的边缘，琐碎的特征。人类可能会开始散居海外，从而使越来越复杂的情报通过银河传播，超越了我们的局限性。 “势力范围”（或某些势力范围将设想“征服的边界”）可能涵盖整个星系，通过自我复制机传播，传输DNA或3-D打印机指令。跃向邻近恒星只是这一过程的第一步。星际航行，甚至星际航行，对近乎永生的人不会有任何恐怖。 
而且，即使使用的唯一推进剂是目前已知的推进剂，从今天算起，这种银河系定殖所需的时间也要比自寒武纪爆发以来已过去的5亿多年要少。如果它相对地发展的话，甚至还不到灵长类动物问世以来的5500万年。
 扩张主义的设想将导致我们的后代变得如此显眼，以至于任何外来文明都会意识到它们。
   我们不知道情报的出现可能需要如此罕见的事件和偶然事件链，例如中奖，这在其他任何地方都没有发生。这将使SETI搜索人员失望，并解释所谓的“费米悖论”（Fermi Paradox），这是物理学家恩里科·费米（Enrico Fermi）对银河系中没有其他智能文明存在的迹象表示惊讶。但是，假设我们并不孤单。我们希望找到什么证据？
  假设确实有许多其他星球出现了生命，而在其中一些星球上，达尔文的演化遵循与地球相似的轨道。即使那样，关键阶段也极不可能同步。如果某个星球上出现的智能技术远远落后于地球上发生的一切（例如，因为该星球更年轻，或者因为某些发展瓶颈需要更长的谈判时间），那么该星球将不会显示出ET的证据。 。在地球生命存在的前20亿年中，地球本身可能不会被视为具有生命的行星。
 但是，在一颗比太阳还老的恒星周围，生命本来可以有十亿年或更长时间的开始。请注意，目前的太阳系年龄大约是我们银河系年龄的一半，也是太阳预计总寿命的一半。我们期望我们银河系中的大部分恒星比太阳更早。
 人类技术文明的历史只有几千年的历史。可能只有几个世纪的时间，人类才能被无机情报超越或超越，然后将继续存在，并在数十亿年的时间里以比达尔文式的速度更快的速度发展。一般而言，有机的人类层面的智能可能只是机器接管之前的短暂插曲，因此，如果外来智能以类似的方式发展，那么我们最不可能在短暂的时间内抓住它形式。如果我们检测到ET，那么在占主导地位的生物不是有血有肉的情况下，甚至可能与行星表面没有联系的情况下，电子化的可能性就更大。
 天文学观测现已使所谓的德雷克方程式（Drake Equation）中的许多概率因素变得神秘，德雷克方程式是传统上用来估算银河系中高级文明数量的概率性尝试。潜在宜居行星的数量已从几十年前完全未知，改为由观测结果直接确定。同时，我们必须重新解释德雷克方程式中的关键因素之一。有机文明的生命最多可能是几千年。但是它的电子侨民可能会持续数十亿年。 
如果SETI成功，则该信号不太可能成为可解码的消息。它更有可能揭示我们所理解的某些超级复杂机器的副产品（甚至是故障）。
 提及“外来文明”的习惯本身可能过于严格。文明意味着个人社会。相反，ET可能是单个集成智能。即使正在传输消息，我们也可能不会认识到它们是虚假的，因为我们可能不知道如何对其进行解码，就像只熟悉幅度调制（AM）传输的资深无线电工程师可能很难理解现代的一样。无线通信。确实，压缩技术旨在使信号尽可能接近噪声。在可预测的信号范围内，还有更多压缩的余地。
 迄今为止，SETI专注于频谱的无线电部分。但是，我们应该探索所有波段，包括光学和X射线波段。我们还应该警惕其他非自然现象或活动的证据。那么什么是相对通用的签名？能源消耗是先进文明的潜在标志之一，似乎很难掩盖。
 星光是可用于先进技术的最合理的长期能源之一。强大的外星文明可能会建立一个巨型结构，称为“戴森球”，以从一颗恒星，许多恒星甚至整个星系中收集恒星能量。
 另一个潜在的长期能源是氢可控地融合成较重的原子核。在这两种情况下，废热和可检测的中红外信号都是不可避免的结果。或者，人们可能会寻找证据证明诸如戴森球体本身之类的巨大文物。有趣的是，值得在我们自己的太阳系中寻找人造物：也许我们可以排除人类规模的外星人的造访，但是如果一个外星文明掌握了纳米技术并将其智能转移到机器上，“入侵”可能包括成群的人类。可能逃避注意的显微镜探针。尽管如此，发送无线电或激光信号比穿越星际空间令人难以置信的距离要容易得多。
  最后，让我们快步前进不仅是几千年，而且是一个天文时间尺度，要长几百万倍。随着星际气体的消耗，我们银河系中恒星诞生和死亡的生态将更加缓慢地发展，直到与仙女座星系碰撞的环境冲击（大约45亿年）震撼为止。我们的银河系仙女座和它们的较小同伴（称为本地群）的碎片将聚集成一个无定形（或椭圆形）的星系。由于宇宙膨胀的加速，遥远的星系将移动得更远，后退的速度越来越快，直到它们消失（就像物体掉入黑洞一样）为止，遇到了一个视野，视线因其超出视野和因果接触而消失了。但是，我们本地小组的残余人员可能会持续更长的时间。足够长的时间可能被称为“ Kardashev III型”现象，在这种现象中，一个文明正在利用一个或多个星系的能量，甚至可能是超大质量黑洞释放的能量，成为长期的高潮生物系统趋向于获得复杂性和负熵（较高阶数）的趋势。
 基本物理学所设定的唯一限制是可到达质子的数量（因为原则上可以将它们转化为任何元素），以及可到达能量的总量（E = mc 2，其中m是质量，c是速度）。光）又可以从一种形式转换为另一种形式。 
本质上，曾经存在于恒星和气体中的所有原子都可以转化为与生物或硅片一样复杂的结构，但具有宇宙规模。一些科幻小说家设想采用恒星级工程来制造黑洞和虫洞，其概念远远超出了我们可以想象的任何技术能力，但并未违反基本物理定律。
 在1960年代初期，在太空竞赛中，美国和苏联科学家都没有真正知道火星或金星之类的行星的位置，特别是在航天器导航所必需的精度和精确度上。听起来有些可笑。他们当然知道...阅读更多
 如果我们想走得更远，那么本地组中的总能量就不是可用资源的限制。对于一个令人难以置信的先进文明来说，将由于空间的宇宙膨胀而消退的星系在它们加速并消失于地平线之前仍然符合物理定律。这样的超智能物种可以将它们拉进去，以构建类似于爱因斯坦最初关于平衡静态宇宙的想法的部分，其平均密度使得暗能量引起的宇宙排斥力在重力的作用下得以精确平衡。
 我们所说的一切都符合我们理解的物理学定律和宇宙学模型。我们的推测是假设引起宇宙加速的排斥力持续存在（并由暗能量或爱因斯坦的宇宙常数描述）。但是我们应该对许多我们不了解的可能性持开放态度。
 自从我们的祖先漫游非洲大草原并应对生活所带来的挑战以来，人类的大脑变化相对较小。确实令人惊讶的是，这些大脑使我们能够理解量子亚原子世界和整个宇宙，而这与我们所进化的日常世界相去甚远。
 科学前沿正在迅速发展。但是我们可能会在某个时候达到缓冲。可能存在某些现象，其中一些对我们的长期命运至关重要，因此，除了大猩猩了解恒星和星系的性质外，我们什么都没有意识到。物理现实可能包含我们的智力和感官都无法把握的复杂性。电子大脑可能对现实有完全不同的看法。因此，我们无法预测甚至无法理解这种大脑的动机。我们无法评估费米悖论是表示他们的缺​​席，还是仅仅表示他们的偏爱。
 关于高级或智能生活的猜想比关于简单生活的猜想更不稳定。然而，有三个特征可以表征SETI搜索可以揭示的实体。
